overview.summary: Implement a query result caching layer for the analytics platform to reduce query response times from 30+ seconds to under 5 seconds for frequently run queries.
goals: Reduce 95th percentile query time to under 5 seconds

functional_requirements.title: Cache query results
functional_requirements.description: When a query is executed, cache the results with a configurable TTL. Subsequent identical queries return cached results.

acceptance_criteria.given: A query that has not been run before
acceptance_criteria.then: Results are computed, returned to user, and stored in cache with default 1-hour TTL

acceptance_criteria.given: A query with cached results within TTL
A query with expired cached results
acceptance_criteria.then: Fresh results are computed and cache is updated

test_hints.focus: Cache key generation is deterministic for identical queries
Cache hit returns correct data matching original query

functional_requirements.title: Query normalization
functional_requirements.description: Normalize queries before cache key generation to maximize cache hits. Equivalent queries with different whitespace or formatting should match.

acceptance_criteria.given: Two queries that differ only in whitespace
acceptance_criteria.then: The second query returns cached results from the first

acceptance_criteria.given: Two queries with different column ordering in SELECT
acceptance_criteria.then: Each query is cached separately (different results)

test_hints.focus: Normalization removes excess whitespace and lowercases keywords
Queries with embedded newlines and tabs

functional_requirements.title: Cache invalidation on data change
functional_requirements.description: When underlying data changes, invalidate affected cached queries to prevent stale results.

acceptance_criteria.given: A cached query result for table X
acceptance_criteria.when: Data in table X is updated
acceptance_criteria.then: The cached result is invalidated within 5 minutes

acceptance_criteria.given: A cached query result for table X
acceptance_criteria.when: Data in unrelated table Y is updated

test_hints.focus: Table dependency tracking identifies affected queries

functional_requirements.uncertainty_reason: Table dependency tracking complexity for complex joins
functional_requirements.discovery_prompt: How should we handle cache invalidation for queries that join 5+ tables?

functional_requirements.title: Cache bypass option
functional_requirements.description: Allow users to bypass cache and force fresh query execution when needed.

acceptance_criteria.then: Fresh results are computed and returned

acceptance_criteria.given: A query executed with NOCACHE hint
acceptance_criteria.then: Results are still stored in cache for future requests

test_hints.focus: NOCACHE hint is parsed correctly from query
Bypass returns fresh data even when cache exists

functional_requirements.title: Cache statistics dashboard
functional_requirements.description: Provide visibility into cache hit rates, memory usage, and query patterns.

acceptance_criteria.then: See cache hit rate, miss rate, eviction count, and memory usage
See most frequently cached queries sorted by hit count

non_functional_requirements.target: Manual cache clear completes in < 30 seconds

checkpoints.name: Core Caching Complete
checkpoints.description: Validate basic caching functionality before building dependent features
validation_criteria: Integration test shows cache hit returns in < 100ms
No data corruption in cached results

checkpoints.name: Full System Ready
checkpoints.description: Validate complete caching system before release
validation_criteria: NFR-001 target met in load test

technical_constraints.constraint: Must use PostgreSQL for cache metadata storage
Cache memory limited to 16GB per node

out_of_scope: Distributed cache across regions (single region only)
Cache warming on startup (cold start acceptable)

glossary.definition: Time To Live - duration a cached entry remains valid before expiration
Query result found in cache and returned without re-execution
Query result not found in cache, requiring fresh execution
